key_insight: HVAF spraying can produce thick NiTi coatings that retain martensitic transformation and shape-memory functionality after annealing, despite a heterogeneous as-sprayed microstructure.
engineering_relevance: This enables durable functional coatings for components needing wear, corrosion, friction, and thermal-actuation benefits beyond conventional hard ceramic coatings.
method: Deposited NiTi on mild steel using HVAF thermal spraying.
Varied four sets of spray processing parameters to study coating quality.
Assessed composition, porosity, microstructure, phase transformation, and thermomechanical response.
evidence_signal: The coatings were evaluated by microscopy and transformation/functional tests, showing martensitic transformation on cooling or loading and actuation in 3-point bending, nanoindentation, and scratch tests.
watch_item: As-sprayed coatings are microstructurally inhomogeneous and require annealing; tensile strength remains limited, so process control and post-treatment are critical.
paper_title: HVAF Spraying of NiTi Coatings: Microstructure, Phase Transformation and Shape Memory Behavior
